user_query:
I'm a science fiction blogger trying to refine my content strategy using past performance and a new draft. Please do two things using the provided local files and save the results under the outputs directory. Inputs: - input/posts.csv — historical post performance with columns: id,title,published_at,tags,words,views,likes,comments,shares - input/draft.html — an HTML draft of an upcoming post Part A — Tag performance summary (write to outputs/tag_summary.csv): - Read input/posts.csv and compute an engagement_rate for each post as (likes + comments + shares) / views. - Treat the tags field as a semicolon-separated list; include a post in each tag’s aggregation when it has multiple tags. - For each tag that appears in at least 2 posts, compute: • post_count (number of posts tagged with it) • median_views • median_engagement_rate • median_words • pct_posts_above_overall_median_engagement = percentage of the tag’s posts whose engagement_rate is greater than or equal to the overall median engagement_rate across all posts - Output a CSV with a header row and the columns in this exact order: tag,post_count,median_views,median_engagement_rate,median_words,pct_posts_above_overall_median_engagement - Sort rows by median_engagement_rate (descending), breaking ties by median_views (descending). Part B — Draft structure extraction (write to outputs/draft_analysis.json): - Parse input/draft.html and analyze only visible text within the
element. - Extract: • total_words: count of words in visible text (exclude tags, scripts, and attributes) • estimated_reading_time_minutes: ceil(total_words / 220) • heading_count_by_level: counts for h1, h2, and h3 in the article • headings_extract: array of objects for each h1–h3 in document order with fields {level, text} • max_heading_depth: the deepest heading level present among h1–h6 in the article (integer 1–6) • images_count: number of elements in the article • total_links: number of elements in the article • outbound_links_by_domain: a mapping of domain to count for all hrefs that are absolute http/https URLs (ignore relative links) - Save outputs/draft_analysis.json as a single JSON object with the above keys. Notes: - Assume views > 0 for all posts. - Use standard median (if even count, average the two middle values). - All outputs must be written to the outputs directory exactly as specified. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
